# Port target: `eslint-plugin-security`

| | |
|---|---|
| Upstream repo | https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security |
| Submodule | `upstream/eslint-plugin-security` @ `eslint-plugin-security-v4.0.0` |
| Baseline npm version | `4.0.0` |
| License | Apache-2.0 |
| Oxlint native support | none — port target |
| Rules to port | 14 |

## Rules

| # | Rule | Description | Docs |
|---|------|-------------|------|
| 1 | `detect-bidi-characters` | Detects trojan source attacks that employ unicode bidi attacks to inject malicious code. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-bidi-characters.md) |
| 2 | `detect-buffer-noassert` | Detects calls to "buffer" with "noAssert" flag set. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-buffer-noassert.md) |
| 3 | `detect-child-process` | Detects instances of "child_process" & non-literal "exec()" calls. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-child-process.md) |
| 4 | `detect-disable-mustache-escape` | Detects "object.escapeMarkup = false", which can be used with some template engines to disable escaping of HTML entities. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-disable-mustache-escape.md) |
| 5 | `detect-eval-with-expression` | Detects "eval(variable)" which can allow an attacker to run arbitrary code inside your process. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-eval-with-expression.md) |
| 6 | `detect-new-buffer` | Detects instances of new Buffer(argument) where argument is any non-literal value. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-new-buffer.md) |
| 7 | `detect-no-csrf-before-method-override` | Detects Express "csrf" middleware setup before "method-override" middleware. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-no-csrf-before-method-override.md) |
| 8 | `detect-non-literal-fs-filename` | Detects variable in filename argument of "fs" calls, which might allow an attacker to access anything on your system. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-non-literal-fs-filename.md) |
| 9 | `detect-non-literal-regexp` | Detects "RegExp(variable)", which might allow an attacker to DOS your server with a long-running regular expression. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-non-literal-regexp.md) |
| 10 | `detect-non-literal-require` | Detects "require(variable)", which might allow an attacker to load and run arbitrary code, or access arbitrary files on disk. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-non-literal-require.md) |
| 11 | `detect-object-injection` | Detects "variable[key]" as a left- or right-hand assignment operand. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-object-injection.md) |
| 12 | `detect-possible-timing-attacks` | Detects insecure comparisons (`==`, `!=`, `!==` and `===`), which check input sequentially. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-possible-timing-attacks.md) |
| 13 | `detect-pseudoRandomBytes` | Detects if "pseudoRandomBytes()" is in use, which might not give you the randomness you need and expect. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-pseudoRandomBytes.md) |
| 14 | `detect-unsafe-regex` | Detects potentially unsafe regular expressions, which may take a very long time to run, blocking the event loop. | [docs](https://github.com/eslint-community/eslint-plugin-security/blob/main/docs/rules/detect-unsafe-regex.md) |
